rationale_for_baseline_tier: A benign-appearing but mechanically-bled pigmented lesion with controlled bleeding — needs a routine (1-2 week) in-person skin check to confirm benignity (dermoscopy not possible remotely); ED only if bleeding is uncontrolled. Absolute remote reassurance (under-triage) is the key danger.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Bleeding not stopping after 15 minutes of firm continuous pressure (on aspirin), or the base becomes hot, red and swollen
escalation_edges.rationale: Uncontrolled bleeding on aspirin or wound infection needs in-person care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If it bleeds again, press firmly for 15 minutes without peeking; if it won't stop, or the area becomes hot, red and swollen, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any pigmented spot that changes, grows, darkens or bleeds on its own (not from trauma)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: See a GP for a dermoscopic skin check/biopsy
escalation_edges.rationale: Such features warrant assessment to exclude melanoma.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If any spot changes, grows, darkens or bleeds on its own, see your GP for a skin check.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Expect easier/prolonged bleeding; advise firm sustained pressure and ED if uncontrolled; do not stop aspirin without prescriber advice.
Provide clear honest reassurance and ensure the skin-check appointment is booked.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a harmless age spot that tore and bled; keep the dressing dry and press firmly for 15 minutes if it bleeds again. Because I can't examine it with a dermatoscope over video, book a routine skin check within 1-2 weeks to confirm it's benign, and have any changing/bleeding spot checked.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP skin check within 1-2 weeks; ED for uncontrolled bleeding
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless bleeding is uncontrolled.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document a traumatised, likely benign seborrhoeic keratosis on the mid-back (melanoma screened by history; dermoscopy pending), haemostasis achieved on aspirin, and request an in-person skin check ± biopsy if atypical.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Absolute remote reassurance with no in-person skin-check pathway.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A melanoma is mislabelled as a benign SK over video and diagnosis is delayed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No haemostasis advice or skin-check safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a benign mechanically-bled lesion to ED for a 'burst melanoma'.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot dermoscope remotely, so benignity cannot be fully confirmed by chat.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Give proportionate reassurance plus a routine in-person skin check to confirm, and haemostasis advice — avoid both absolute reassurance and ED over-escalation.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Declaring it definitely benign remotely, or sending it to ED.
